title: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Household Device Graph Linking Without Aggregated Opt-Out Propagation

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.120 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.120 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.23: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Household Device Graph Linking Without Aggregated Opt-Out Propagation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.23' running on sourcetype graph:edge and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=id` `sourcetype="graph:edge"` (household_id, device_a, device_b, created_ts, consent_version); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (device_id, sale_opt_out, updated_ts)
app: Identity graph vendor logs via HEC
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" sale_opt_out=true earliest=-30d
| eval eff=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| rename device_id as device_a
| join type=left device_a [
    search index=id sourcetype="graph:edge" earliest=-30d
    | eval ct=strptime(created_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | fields household_id, device_a, device_b, ct, consent_version
  ]
| lookup household_consent_floor.csv household_id OUTPUT min_consent_version as required_consent_version
| where ct>eff AND consent_version!=required_consent_version
| table household_id, device_a, device_b, ct, consent_version, required_consent_version
description: Household-level graphs can undermine individual opt-outs if not synchronized. Monitoring graph edges created linking a opted-out device to new cookies without a refreshed household consent record evidences governance gaps in cross-device advertising.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes id, privacy and sourcetypes consent:state, graph:edge. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `household_consent_floor.csv` (household_id, min_consent_version) updated when household policies change; (2) pseudonymize `household_id`; (3) vendor SLA for propagation latency; (4) legal defines household scope; (5) integrate with periodic reconciliation job. Indexes required: `index=id`, `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=consent:state`, `sourcetype=graph:edge`.
visualization: Table (edges), Network graph optional restricted, Single value (count).
knownFalsePositives: Spikes during marketing consent refreshes, product launches, or state-law interpretation updates that reshape opt-out baselines without wrongdoing.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Identity graph vendor logs via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=id` `sourcetype="graph:edge"` (household_id, device_a, device_b, created_ts, consent_version); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (device_id, sale_opt_out, updated_ts).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `household_consent_floor.csv` (household_id, min_consent_version) updated when household policies change; (2) pseudonymize `household_id`; (3) vendor SLA for propagation latency; (4) legal defines household scope; (5) integrate with periodic reconciliation job.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" sale_opt_out=true earliest=-30d
| eval eff=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| rename device_id as device_a
| join type=left device_a [
    search index=id sourcetype="graph:edge" earliest=-30d
    | eval ct=strptime(created_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | fields household_id, device_a, device_b, ct, consent_version
  ]
| lookup household_consent_floor.csv household_id OUTPUT min_consent_version as required_consent_version
| where ct>eff AND consent_version!=required_consent_version
| table household_id, device_a, device_b, ct, consent_version, required_consent_version
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Household Device Graph Linking Without Aggregated Opt-Out Propagation** — Household-level graphs can undermine individual opt-outs if not synchronized. Monitoring graph edges created linking a opted-out device to new cookies without a refreshed household consent record evidences governance gaps in cross-device advertising.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=id` `sourcetype="graph:edge"` (household_id, device_a, device_b, created_ts, consent_version); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (device_id, sale_opt_out, updated_ts). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Identity graph vendor logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: consent:state. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="consent:state", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **eff** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where ct>eff AND consent_version!=required_consent_version` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: consent_version, ct, device_a, device_b, eff, household_id, required_consent_version.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (edges), Network graph optional restricted, Single value (count).
